rulings.Case Title: DALE RILEY, SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P): AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT PETER KIEWIT SONS', INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff failed to present evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact given plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories and the deposition testimony. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARK BROWN et al VS. ANTIQUE ENTERPRISES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order In Aid Of Appraisal Reference (Corp Code 17351)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 2. PLAINTIFFS MARK BROWN AND DEIRDRE BROWN'S Motion For Order In Aid Of Appraisal Reference (Corp Code 17351). A hearing is required. The motion is granted. The Court adopts plaintiffs' method for the selection of the 3 appraisers. The Court adopts August 18, 2008 as the valuation date. The parties may agree to an alternate date. The parties are to meet and confer to set a discovery schedule and/or agree to an exchange of documents and depositions if necessary. The parties are also to agree on a schedule for briefing and hearing the lease issues and valuation method, if necessary. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BD. SAN FRANCISCO,CA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 3. PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN is denied. A judgment was entered and served on plaintiff in January 2008. If this is a motion for reconsideration, it is untimely under CCP 657. If it is a motion for a new trial, it is untimely under CCP 659. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BD. SAN FRANCISCO,CA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 3. PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB'S Motion For Genuiness Of Document is denied. A judgment was entered and served on plaintiff in January 2008. If this is a motion for reconsideration, it is untimely under CCP 657. If it is a motion for a new trial, it is untimely under CCP 659. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NIMER MASSIS et al VS. FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANTS FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff may not rely on Insurance Code 790.03 to plead a cause of action under B & P Code 17200. AICCO, Inc. v Insurance Co. of North America (2001) 90 Cal. App. 4th 579, 596-597. The 6th cause of action makes clear that the policy did not provide wrongful eviction coverage and so there is no covenant to breach. Plaintiff does not oppose the demurrer to the 7th cause of action for reformation. A negligence claim cannot be based on the facts alleged. Brown v. Guarantee Ins. Co. (1957) 155 Cal. App. 2d 679, 687-688. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD E. GRAMMENS VS. NEWMARK PACIFIC, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS NEWMARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Pltf'S Fourth Cause Of Action; Appendix Of Secondary Authority In Support Of Its Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Pltf'S Fourth Cause Of Action; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Its Motion For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT NEWMARK PACIFIC, INC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Plaintiff's Fourth Cause Of Action is off calendar. Motion to be made before the trial judge given the current trial date. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES EHRLICH et al VS. MYCELIA NETWORKS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS MYCELIA NETWORKS, INC AND JOSHUA KINBERG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled with respect to the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action. On demurrer, the Court may not consider evidence. The pleading sufficiently alleges a breach of contract. The order on the preliminary injunction does not preclude plaintiff from alleging the 4th and 5th causes of action. The issues raised by defendant must be raised by summary judgment/summary adjudication. The demurrer is sustained as to the 3rd cause of action for failure to allege facts that establish a basis for a duty of care. Ten days leave to amend. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: EMMANUEL ADDO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 11, 2008. rulings.Case Title: SALVADOR CHAVEZ et al VS. PAUL J TUREK DR
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT PAUL TUREK, MD'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action and the reference to Virginia Rojas. As to Jennie Chavez claim for medical malpractice, the motion is granted. If Ms. Chavez desires to pursue this claim, she should do so by filing a motion seeking leave to amend. It appears from the 3rd amended complaint that there is a basis for her to plead such a claim. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. ROSA M. TARDENCILLA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 10. PLAINTIFF LVNV FUNDING LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: LOC KHUU VS. KATHERINE DELMAR BURKE SCHOOL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT KATHERINE DELMAR BURKE SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. A single act may be found to constitute an unfair business practice based on the 1992 amendment. Under Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products, Co. (2000) 23 Cal. 4th 163, 178, plaintiff could be entitled to restitution. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ASIAN, INC et al VS. THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Deft Nonprofits Insurance Alliance Of California'S Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Its Demurrer To Asian Inc. And 1030 Polk Associates' Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT NONPROFITS INSURANCE ALLIANCE GROUP'S Demurrer To Asian Inc And 1030 Polk Associates' Complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st, 2nd and 6th causes of action. Plaintiff must attach the policy or policies at issue or plead more specifically the coverage at issue. Plaintiff also must plead separate causes of action for first party and third party liability. The fraud claim lacks the requisite specificity. The reformation claim fails to plead mutual mistake. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MARK O'FLYNN et al VS. SUSAN SUVAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 18, 2008, line 18. DEFENDANTS SUSAN SUVAL AND REBECCA SUVAL'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is denied. The complaint pleads a valid cause of action. It is not possible to resolve the merits of the cause of action based on defendants' evidence. As a consequence, plaintiff's alleged compliance is sufficient as a matter of pleading. =(302/PJM/VC)
